LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS TO EXHIBITS A AND B UNDER EVID. CODE 452(D). JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED TO EXHIBITS C-G ONLY TO THE EXTENT THAT THE DOCUMENTS EXIST AND THE PARTIES INVOLVED. THE CONTENT OF THE DOCUMENTS IS ARE NOT CAPABLE OF IMMEDIATE AND ACCURATE DETERMINATION UNDER EVID. CODE 452(H). THE COURT VACATES ITS ORDER OF JULY 17, 2013, AS THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF FILED THE AC ON JULY 16, 2013 AS A MATTER OF RIGHT UNDER CCP 472. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION ON STATUE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES FACTS THAT IT DID NOT DISCOVER DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT UNTIL DEFENDANT MET WITH KIM FOX-YODER AND AN OUTSIDE ACCOUNTANT ON OR AROUND JULY 2012 AND DEFENDANT RETURNED HIS WORK LAPTOP. (AC 23-25.) DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS THAT PLAINTIFF' FAILED TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT PLAINTIFF HAD ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIPS WITH THIRD PARTIES THAT WERE DISRUPTED. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS, IF IT CAN SO IN GOOD FAITH, REGARDING THE THIRD PARTY OR PARTIES THAT DEFENDANT MISDIRECTED BUSINESS FROM AND THE BUSINESS RELATIONSHIPS DEFENDANT DISRUPTED. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD FACTS WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY REQUIRED FOR A FRAUD CLAIM. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD FACTS REGARDING DEFENDANT'S INTENT TO INDUCE PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE AND PLAINTIFF'S SUBSTANTIAL RELIANCE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO T

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SUBSTANTIVELY ADDRESS THE MERITS OF HIS MOTION TO STRIKE IN THE MOVING MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. INSTEAD, DEFENDANT ADDRESSES THE MERITS OF THE MOTION, FOR THE FIRST TIME, IN HIS REPLY BRIEF. (SEE BALBOA INS. CO. V. AGUIRRE (1983) 149 CAL.APP.3D 1002, 1010.) FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT IMPROPERLY INCLUDES LEGAL ARGUMENT IN HIS NOTICE OF MOTION. 1 ORDER FOR BOTH MOTIONS ON CALENDAR SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER, BOTH INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA CAL-FAB AND DBA DW WIRE CLOTH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1-4. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER PLAINTIFF AUTHORIZED DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT AND WHETHER DEFENDANT INTERFERED WITH PLAINTIFF'S CUSTOMER RELATIONSHIPS. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 5-8. THE DECLARATION OF SUSAN SALAMONE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT PURCHASED PRODUCTS/EQUIPMENT ON PLAINTIFF'S BEHALF, BUT PLAINTIFF NEVER RECEIVED IT. THE TRIER OF FACT COULD INFER THE FRAUDULENT INTENT BASED ON DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT. THE COURT REJECTS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT. AT PAR. 12 OF THE SALMONE DEC., PLAINTIFF ATTACHES INVOICES FROM 2011 AND 2012 THAT SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF ALLEGEDLY PURCHASED SOME FRAMES BUT THEY WERE NEVER RECEIVED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUE 9. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE CUSTOMER LIST QUALIFIES AS A TRADE SECRET. (SALAMONE DEC., PARS. 9-10). THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUE 10. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT SANCTIONED PLAINTIFF'S CONDUCT AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO AN ACCOUNTING. MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER, both individually and dba CAL-FAB and dba DW WIRE CLOTH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied in its entirety. The motion is denied as to issues 1-4. There is a triable issue of material fact whether plaintiff authorized defendant's conduct and whether defendant interfered with plaintiff's customer relationships. The motion is denied as to issues 5-8. The declaration of Susan Salamone shows that defendant purchased products/equipment on plaintiff's behalf, but plaintiff never received it. The trier of fact could infer the fraudulent intent based on defendant's conduct. The court rejects the statute of limitations argument. At par. 12 of the Salmone dec., plaintiff attaches invoices from 2011 and 2012 that show that plaintiff allegedly purchased some frames but they were never received. The motion is denied as to issue 9. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the customer list qualifies as a trade secret. (Salamone dec., pars. 9-10). The motion is denied as to issue 10. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant sanctioned plaintiff's conduct and whether plaintiff is entitled to an accounting. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, TEL. NO. (415)717-9768 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-13-2014 Continued To Aug-13-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION To Strike Filed Concurrently With Defendants Demurrer As To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint And Proof Of Service
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S MOTION To Strike is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A-C and H, and denied as to all other exhibits. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit A. The declarations and attached exhibits submitted by both parties were not considered in ruling on this motion to strike, as a motion to strike can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or matters that are judicially noticeable. (CCP sec. 437(a).) Although Plaintiff amended its complaint beyond the 10 days allowed in this Court's previous order, it serves judicial economy to allow the amendments to stand. Defendant has not shown that he is prejudiced by the untimely amendment. There is no trial date set. Therefore, if Plaintiff would have sought leave to amend after the 10 days expired, such relief would have been granted, given the strong policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. (See e.g., Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Defendant's motion to strike the references in the Second Amended Complaint to Defendant being a vice president or executive is also denied. An individual could hold the titles of operations manager, vice president, and executive at the same time. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A-C and H, and denied as to all other exhibits. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit A. The declarations and attached exhibits submitted by both parties were not considered in ruling on this demurrer, as a demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or matters that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) As to the first and second causes of action, the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately pleads the elements of intentional and negligent interference with prospective business advantage and the allegations are not impermissibly inconsistent with earlier pleadings. As to the third cause of action for fraud, the SAC adequately pleads justifiable reliance with the requisite specificity. (SAC, par. 43.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.

Defendant Darren Welter'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Darren Welter's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint is denied. Defendant fails to substantively address the merits of his motion to strike in the moving Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Instead, Defendant addresses the merits of the motion, for the first time, in his Reply brief. (See Balboa Ins. Co. v. Aguirre (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 1002, 1010.) Furthermore, Defendant improperly includes legal argument in his notice of motion. 1 ORDER FOR BOTH MOTIONS ON CALENDAR SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Defendant Darren Welter'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Darren Welter's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted as to Exhibits A and B under Evid. Code 452(d). Judicial Notice is granted to Exhibits C-G only to the extent that the documents exist and the parties involved. The content of the documents is are not capable of immediate and accurate determination under Evid. Code 452(h). The Court vacates its Order of July 17, 2013, as the Register of Actions shows that Plaintiff filed the AC on July 16, 2013 as a matter of right under CCP 472. Defendant's demurrer to each cause of action on statue of limitations grounds is overruled. Plaintiff alleges facts that it did not discover Defendant's alleged wrongful conduct until Defendant met with Kim Fox-Yoder and an outside accountant on or around July 2012 and Defendant returned his work laptop. (AC 23-25.)
Defendant's demurrer on the grounds that Plaintiff' failed to allege sufficient facts to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend as to the first, second, and third causes of action, and overruled as to the Fourth, Fifth and Sixth causes of action. As to the first and second causes of action, Plaintiff fails to allege sufficient facts that Plaintiff had economic relationships with third parties that were disrupted. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to plead facts, if it can so in good faith, regarding the third party or parties that Defendant misdirected business from and the business relationships Defendant disrupted. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff fails to plead facts with the requisite specificity required for a fraud claim. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to plead facts regarding Defendant's intent to induce Plaintiff's reliance and Plaintiff's substantial reliance. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action are overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim.
Defendant's demurrer to each cause of action on the grounds of uncertainty is overruled as the facts allow Defendant to reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against him. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) 1 Order FOR BOTH MOTIONS ON CALENDAR signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
